assertions.title: Disable MFA for User

steps.action: Inspect user rows for MFA badge/icon
steps.expected: At least one user with MFA enabled
steps.observed: No MFA badges or icons visible on any user row

steps.action: Click action menu on first user (Validation Admin)
steps.expected: Action dropdown with Disable MFA option
steps.observed: Dropdown shows: View, Edit, Suspend, Force reset password, Delete — no Disable MFA option

steps.expected: Disable MFA option available for MFA-enabled user
steps.observed: No user has MFA enabled; Disable MFA option not present in any action menu

assertions.issues: Blocked: No test users have MFA enabled in seed data. The Disable MFA action only appears for users with MFA active. No MFA badges visible on any user row.

assertions.title: Changelog Management Lists Entries

steps.expected: Changelog entries listed with version, date, content preview
steps.observed: Page renders with empty state: 'No entries yet' and 'Check back after the next release.'

steps.action: Check for changelog entries in data
steps.expected: At least one changelog entry from backend seed data
steps.observed: No entries exist. Page shows informational message: 'Changelog entries are managed by the platform team.'

evidence.network: N/A — page correctly renders empty state

assertions.issues: Blocked: No changelog entries exist in the backend. Page correctly renders an empty state with descriptive message. Cannot verify listing behavior without seed data.

assertions.title: WhatsNew Banner Displays for Unread Changelog

steps.expected: WhatsNew banner or indicator visible if unread changelog exists
steps.observed: No WhatsNew banner or indicator visible on dashboard

steps.action: Check /admin/changelog for entries that would trigger banner
steps.observed: No changelog entries exist at all — cannot trigger WhatsNew banner

evidence.network: N/A — banner requires changelog entries to trigger

assertions.issues: Blocked: Requires changelog entries to trigger the WhatsNew banner. Since no changelog entries exist in seed data (see VAL-ADMIN-018), the banner cannot be tested.

assertions.title: Audit Trail Lists Business Events

steps.expected: Business events listed with timestamp, actor, action, resource
steps.observed: Page shows error state: 'Audit Service Unavailable — The audit trail service is experiencing issues on the backend.'

steps.action: Direct API call to backend: GET /api/v1/audit/business-events?page=0&size=20

assertions.issues: Blocked: Backend GET /api/v1/audit/business-events returns HTTP 500. Same issue as Round 2. The backend audit service is broken (Docker-managed, not modified). Frontend correctly shows error state with retry option.

assertions.title: Tenant Runtime View Shows Active Tenants

steps.expected: Active tenants with resource usage and connection status
steps.observed: Page shows 'Failed to load runtime metrics.' with Retry button

steps.action: Direct API call to backend: GET /api/v1/admin/tenant-runtime/metrics
Try with X-Company-Code header instead of X-Company-Id
steps.observed: Still returns 404 — endpoint does not exist on backend

assertions.issues: Blocked: Backend endpoint GET /api/v1/admin/tenant-runtime/metrics returns 404. The endpoint does not exist in the Docker-managed backend. Same issue as Round 2. Note: Security Policy section of the page does render correctly with session timeout, password rules, MFA policy — but the Runtime Metrics section cannot load.

blockers.description: VAL-ADMIN-008: No test users have MFA enabled in seed data. Cannot test Disable MFA action.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Checked all 7 users in the users list — none have MFA badge or icon

blockers.description: VAL-ADMIN-018/VAL-ADMIN-020: No changelog entries in backend seed data. Cannot test listing or WhatsNew banner.
VAL-ADMIN-026: Backend GET /api/v1/audit/business-events returns HTTP 500. Audit service is broken.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Retried 3 times via UI and direct curl — all return 500

blockers.description: VAL-ADMIN-028: Backend GET /api/v1/admin/tenant-runtime/metrics returns HTTP 404. Endpoint does not exist.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Tried with X-Company-Code header — still 404. Endpoint confirmed non-existent on backend.

summary: Tested 5 assertions in Round 3 re-check: all 5 remain blocked with the same issues as Round 2. No backend changes detected (Docker-managed). VAL-ADMIN-008 blocked by missing MFA-enabled test users. VAL-ADMIN-018/020 blocked by missing changelog seed data. VAL-ADMIN-026 blocked by backend 500 on audit endpoint. VAL-ADMIN-028 blocked by backend 404 (endpoint doesn't exist). Frontend error handling is appropriate for all blocked cases.
